student_message: Line of symmetry! V has two lines. Next!
final_message:
You got the riddle exactly right! It is a line of symmetry. Let's look really closely at the letter $$V$$, though. If we fold it right down the middle from top to bottom, the left and right sides match perfectly. But if we try folding it across from side to side, the top and bottom don't match at all. So, the letter $$V$$ actually just has one vertical line of symmetry! 

Let's try looking at another letter together. Manu says the letter $$N$$ has a vertical line of symmetry because it looks balanced and tall. Do you think Manu is correct? Tell me why or why not! 🤔✨

orchestrator_intent.goal: Correct the error about V having two lines, then give a quiz.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The student correctly identified the line of symmetry but incorrectly stated the letter V has two lines. This misconception must be corrected before advancing to the next question.

current_section_title: Reflection Symmetry and Lines of Symmetry
current_section_content: A line of symmetry is a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry (left-right mirror), some have a horizontal line (top-bottom mirror), and some have both. You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
